column266: if the patient has stage iiib or iiic melanoma, the patient usually has clinically detectable lymph nodes confirmed as malignant on pathology report and/or ulcerated primary lesions
column267: the patient with stage iii melanoma may have 'n2c' classification with 2-3 metastatic nodes and in transit metastases/satellites without metastatic nodes, or 'n3' classification with any 't' and 4 or more metastatic nodes, or matted nodes, or in transit metastases/satellites with metastatic nodes
column268: the pathology report for both stage iiib and iiic must be reviewed, signed, and dated by the investigator
column269: clinically detectable lymph nodes are defined as a palpable node confirmed as malignant by pathology, a non-palpable but enlarged lymph node by ct scan (at least 15 mm in short axis) confirmed as malignant by pathology, a pet scan positive lymph node of any size confirmed by pathology, or evidence of pathologically macrometastatic disease in one or more lymph nodes defined by one or more foci of melanoma at least 1 cm in diameter
column270: if the patient has stage iv melanoma, the pathology report confirming negative margins must be reviewed, dated, and signed by the investigator prior to randomization
column271: the patient has disease-free status documented by a complete physical examination and imaging studies within 4 weeks prior to randomization
column272: the patient has had imaging studies including a ct scan of the neck, chest, abdomen, pelvis, and all known sites of resected disease, and a brain mri or ct (if mri is contraindicated or there is no known history of resected brain lesions)
column273: the patient can provide tumor tissue from the resected site of disease for biomarker analyses
column274: the patient has a pd-l1 expression classification (positive, negative, or indeterminate) as determined by a central lab in order to be randomized
column275: the patient has signed the informed consent form
column276: the patient is able to comply with the study protocol, in the investigator’s judgment
column277: the patient has histologically confirmed stage iv (metastatic) or unresectable stage iiic (locally advanced) melanoma, as defined by the american joint committee on cancer, 7th revised edition
column278: the patient is naïve to prior systemic anti-cancer therapy for melanoma, including chemotherapy, hormonal therapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy, or other biologic therapies, except for specified neoadjuvant or adjuvant treatments
column279: the patient has discontinued neoadjuvant or adjuvant chemotherapy at least 28 days prior to initiation of study treatment, if applicable
column280: the patient has discontinued adjuvant treatment with interferon (ifn), interleukin-2 (il-2), or vaccine therapies at least 28 days prior to initiation of study treatment, if applicable
column281: the patient has discontinued adjuvant treatment with herbal therapies at least 7 days prior to initiation of study treatment, if applicable
column282: the patient has adequate hematologic and end-organ function as defined by laboratory test results obtained within 14 days prior to initiation of study treatment, except for amylase, lipase, and ldh where up to 28 days is acceptable
column283: numerical: min white blood cell count (10^9/liter)
column287: numerical: max total bilirubin level (times upper limit of normal)
column288: numerical: max aspartate aminotransferase level (times upper limit of normal)
column289: numerical: max alanine aminotransferase level (times upper limit of normal)
column290: numerical: max amylase level (times upper limit of normal)
column291: numerical: max lipase level (times upper limit of normal)
column292: for patients with documented liver or bone metastases, alkaline phosphatase level less than or equal to 5 times the upper limit of normal
column293: numerical: max international normalized ratio (times upper limit of normal)
column294: numerical: max activated partial thromboplastin time (times upper limit of normal)
column295: the patient receiving therapeutic anticoagulation has a stable anticoagulant regimen and stable inr during the 28 days immediately preceding initiation of study treatment
column296: the female patient of childbearing potential agrees to remain abstinent or use a contraceptive method with a failure rate of less than 1% per year during the treatment period and for 6 months after the last dose of study treatment
column297: the female patient of childbearing potential agrees to refrain from donating eggs during the treatment period and for 6 months after the last dose of study treatment
column298: the male patient agrees to remain abstinent or use contraceptive measures with female partners of childbearing potential, using a condom plus an additional contraceptive method with a failure rate of less than 1% per year during the treatment period and for at least 6 months after the last dose of study treatment
column299: the male patient agrees to refrain from donating sperm during the treatment period and for 6 months after the last dose of study treatment
column300: the male patient with a pregnant female partner agrees to remain abstinent or use a condom during the treatment period and for 6 months after the last dose of study treatment to avoid exposing the embryo
column301: the patient has histologically confirmed unresectable stage iii or stage iv melanoma according to the ajcc staging system
column302: the patient may have received prior adjuvant or neoadjuvant melanoma therapy if it was completed at least 6 weeks before the first dose and all related adverse events have returned to baseline or stabilized
column303: the patient has measurable disease as defined by response evaluation criteria in solid tumors version 1.1 (recist)
column304: the patient can provide tumor tissue from an unresectable or metastatic site of disease for biomarker analyses
column305: the patient must be classified as pd-l1 positive, pd-l1 negative, or pd-l1 indeterminate to be randomized
column306: the patient must consent to the acquisition of additional tumor tissue for biomarker analyses if an insufficient amount is available prior to screening
column307: the patient is eligible regardless of braf status (braf wild type or braf 600 mutation positive)
column308: the patient has histologically confirmed melanoma of cutaneous origin
column309: the patient without clinical or radiologic evidence of regional lymph node involvement must undergo sentinel lymph node biopsy
column310: the patient must undergo a complete regional lymphadenectomy if a sentinel lymph node biopsy cannot be performed or a sentinel lymph node cannot be detected
column311: the patient with clinical or radiographic evidence of regional lymph node involvement or evidence of melanoma involvement in the sentinel lymph node must undergo complete regional lymphadenectomy
column312: the patient must have histologically confirmed melanoma infiltration of lymph node(s)
column313: the patient with lymph node involvement either at initial presentation or a first metachronous nodal recurrence is eligible
column314: the patient has brafv600 mutation–positive cutaneous melanoma (either pathologic stage iic or stage iii according to ajcc staging criteria v7) that has been completely resected
column315: numerical: min lymph node metastasis diameter (mm)
column316: the patient has brafv600 mutation status of the current primary tumor or involved lymph node determined to be positive using the cobas braf v600 mutation test
column317: numerical: max time from surgery to randomization (days)
column318: the patient is able and willing to give signed informed consent prior to performance of any study-related procedures and to comply with the study protocol
column320: the patient must have fully recovered from the effects of any major surgery (including complete regional lymphadenectomy) or significant traumatic injury prior to the first dose of study drug
column322: the patient with a positive stool occult blood test must be cleared for study inclusion by a gastroenterologist or appropriately trained designee
column323: the patient with a known personal history of adenomatous colorectal polyps or colorectal cancer, family history of colon cancer in which a first and/or second degree relative has been diagnosed with colorectal cancer at or after the age of 60 years, or signs or symptoms that could be related to colon cancer as determined by the site investigator or designee, must undergo a screening colonoscopy with adequate resection of all visualized polyps
column324: the patient requiring a screening colonoscopy must have the procedure complete to the cecum, with adequate bowel preparation, and performed within the 90 day screening period
column325: the patient requiring a screening colonoscopy is not required to have it if colonoscopy to the cecum with adequate bowel preparation and adequate resection of all visualized polyps was performed within 1 year of the start of the 90 day screening period, unless the site investigator deems it necessary
column326: the patient with a history of colon cancer greater than 5 years prior to randomization is eligible
column327: the patient has adequate hematologic, liver, and renal function as defined by the following laboratory results obtained within 28 days prior to randomization
column328: numerical: max pt (times upper limit of normal)
column329: numerical: max inr (times upper limit of normal)
column330: numerical: max aptt (times upper limit of normal)
column331: the female patient of childbearing potential and male patient with partners of childbearing potential must agree to always use two effective forms of contraception from the informed consent signature date until at least 6 months after completion of study therapy
column332: the female patient of childbearing potential is defined as a sexually mature woman without prior hysterectomy who has had any evidence of menses within the past 12 months
column333: the female patient is considered not of childbearing potential if amenorrhea for a period of 12 months or longer has occurred in the absence of chemotherapy, antiestrogens, or ovarian suppression
column334: the female patient of childbearing potential must have a negative serum pregnancy test within 14 days prior to randomization
column335: the woman of non childbearing potential does not need to undergo pregnancy testing
column336: the patient must not have any psychological, familial, sociological, or geographical condition that has the potential to hamper compliance with the study protocol and follow up schedule
column337: the patient has histologically confirmed stage iii (unresectable) or stage iv melanoma according to the american joint committee on cancer (ajcc) staging manual (8th edition)
column338: the patient is treatment naïve, with no prior systemic anticancer therapy for unresectable or metastatic melanoma
column339: the patient may have received prior adjuvant or neoadjuvant melanoma therapy in the setting of completely resected disease if it was completed at least 6 months prior to randomization and all related adverse events have either returned to baseline or stabilized
column340: the patient has at least one measurable lesion by computed tomography (ct) or magnetic resonance imaging (mri) according to recist v1.1 criteria
column341: the patient is able to provide tumor tissue (biopsy or surgical specimen, where applicable) from an unresectable or metastatic site of disease for biomarker analyses
column342: the patient must be classified as pd-l1 positive (greater than or equal to 1% tumor cell membrane staining), pd-l1 negative (less than 1% tumor cell membrane staining), or pd-l1 indeterminate (tumor cell membrane scoring hampered by high cytoplasmic staining or melanin content)
column343: the patient must provide a fresh biopsy during the screening phase for biomarker analyses if an insufficient amount of tumor tissue from an unresectable or metastatic site is available prior to the start of the screening phase
column344: the patient must have known braf v600 mutation status or consent to braf v600 mutation testing per local institutional standards during the screening period and have results of braf testing available prior to randomization
column345: the patient with indeterminate braf status or with braf status not documented prior to randomization is not permitted to randomize to a treatment arm
column346: the patient must have completed any prior radiotherapy at least 2 weeks prior to study treatment administration
column347: the patient must be able to swallow pills intact
column349: the patient is undergoing mohs micrographic surgery
column351: the patient has basal cell carcinoma or squamous cell carcinoma
column352: the patient is receiving care at hmc, vasser brothers medical center, or emory university school of medicine
column353: the patient has melanoma or breast cancer with a planned standard of care sentinel node biopsy
column354: the patient with melanoma has the melanoma located on the upper extremity between and including the wrist and shoulder, or on the lower extremity between and including the ankle and groin
column355: the patient is fluent in written and spoken english
column359: stage iv melanoma according to ajcc 2010 criteria
column360: eastern cooperative oncology group performance status of 0 or 1
